claim: Agent behavior is non-deterministic and emergent; traces (not code) are the source of truth for debugging agent logic, replacing stack traces as the primary debugging artifact
actionable: Prioritize trace collection and inspection over code inspection when debugging agents; treat traces as your primary source of truth for understanding what the agent actually did
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Observability for agents requires tracking runs (single LLM calls), traces (full agent execution), and threads (multi-turn conversations with human intervention); each level reveals different failure modes
actionable: Implement tracing at all three levels: capture single tool calls, full agent runs, and conversation history; don't rely on just end-to-end metrics
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Single-step evals (does the agent make the right decision at this moment?) are easiest to define but go stale when agent internals change; full-turn evals are harder to define but capture real behavior
actionable: Start with full-turn evals built from production traces; add single-step evals only after agent architecture stabilizes; this avoids eval maintenance overhead during active development
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Production is where you discover what to evaluate; ship early to small user cohorts to surface real failure modes, then build evals around those observed behaviors
actionable: Deploy agents to a small cohort first, collect traces, identify failure patterns, then build metrics around those patterns; don't pre-emptively build comprehensive evals before seeing production behavior
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Building test sets from production traces (backward) is more effective than pre-defining test cases; users reveal unexpected agent behaviors that wouldn't have been anticipated
actionable: Extract test cases from production traces after failures occur; prioritize real user inputs and failure modes over synthetic test data created without observing actual usage
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Agents commonly exhibit pathological behaviors (e.g., editing the same file repeatedly) detectable via traces; deterministic hooks (middlewares) can inject recovery logic when pathologies are triggered
actionable: Observe agent traces to identify failure patterns (infinite loops, repetitive edits); implement deterministic middleware checks to interrupt pathological behavior and force re-planning
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Online evals (after production runs) use different metrics than offline evals (no ground truth); instead, monitor for tool call patterns, unusual outputs, and failure signals in user feedback
actionable: For online evals, use heuristic evaluators: flag repeated tool calls, excessive output length, hallucinations (content not in input context), or user rejection signals
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Ad hoc insights analysis on trace data (clustering similar failures, comparing clusters) reveals patterns like 'users frustrated with X type of question' without pre-defining metrics
actionable: Use unsupervised clustering on traces to discover patterns; ask exploratory questions about your trace corpus (e.g., which question types fail most?), then define metrics around discoveries
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Traces enable offline eval workflows: find production failure in trace -> extract agent state at failure point -> use as single-step test case -> iterate prompt/tool changes -> re-test
actionable: Create a loop: observe production failures in traces -> snapshot agent state -> build offline test case -> fix prompt -> verify fix -> deploy; repeat systematically
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Manual debugging via traces is valuable even if only used 5% of the time; playground inspection of individual runs within a trace enables rapid hypothesis testing
actionable: Always enable tracing in development; even infrequent trace inspection catches bugs faster than code-level debugging; use playgrounds to test variations on a specific run
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation

claim: Agentic operations will be a new discipline requiring subject matter experts (not traditional DevOps) who can recognize when 'the agent said X and should have said Y' based on domain knowledge
actionable: Staff agentic systems with domain experts who can interpret agent failures; don't expect traditional DevOps skills to transfer to evaluating agent reasoning
source_title: Building Better AI Agents: Observability and Evaluation
